title: Mildly Smoky Egyptian Black Bean Stew
description: A smoky and flavorful one-pot Egyptian dish featuring black beans and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a chilly evening, this plant-based friendly stew is made with a tadka for added depth
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat and add cumin seeds and coriander seeds, letting them sizzle for a few seconds
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine
Add the soaked and drained black beans, canned diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth to the pot
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 30 minutes, or until the beans are tender
Use an immersion blender to puree a portion of the stew, or leave it chunky, as desired
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or a dollop of yogurt, if desired
